items.title: Human bones found on Rottnest Island, investigation underway

children.content: Police are investigating the origin of what are believed to be several human bones which were found on Rottnest Island.

gallery.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.

thumbnail.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.

items.title: Drunk driver jailed over hit-and-run death in Mandurah

image.altText: A smiling young woman with short dark hair and glasses, dressed in black, sits on a bench chair against a wall with artwork.

children.content: An intoxicated driver who killed a woman in a hit-and-run in Mandurah, and was allowed to drive two months later, is sentenced to more than four years in prison.

items.title: Pet dog survives being dragged into remote lake by freshwater crocodile

children.content: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

image.altText: man holding a dog

items.title: FOGO waste collection dubbed 'expensive failure'

children.content: Western Australia's second largest city is investigating the viability of the FOGO waste system amid rising costs, with more than a year's worth of waste trucked to landfill.

image.altText: Residential council bins with City of Bunbury branding

items.title: Vehicle rollover on WA mine site kills worker, seriously injures another

children.content: A 38-year-old man dies in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

image.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'

children.content: Australian seafood prices are likely to rise due to fuel supply issues, with some warning that wild-caught seafood could become unaffordable for most.

image.altText: Prawns at a seafood market

items.title: Court sets aside convictions of parents for starving daughter, grants bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

gallery.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.
A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets
Three women with blurred faces walk down stairs outside a court. One is young and two are older.

thumbnail.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.
A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets
Three women with blurred faces walk down stairs outside a court. One is young and two are older.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody on war crimes charges

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail and a court adjourned his criminal case until June.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's fight to clear his name just got more perilous

children.content: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

gallery.altText: a man wearing a suit arriving at a court and looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: a man wearing a suit arriving at a court and looking at the camera

items.title: ASX enjoys $80 billion rally, oil falls back below $US100 on US-Iran ceasefire — as it happened

children.content: The news of a US-Iran ceasefire in the Perisan Gulf sparked a big rally on Asian share markets, with the ASX up almost 3 per cent while oil prices plummeted.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: Three-day hearing set for Grace Tame abuser over alleged 'menacing' social posts

children.content: Nicolaas Bester, who was previously found guilty of sexually abusing Grace Tame, has been deemed unfit to stand trial over allegations he made "menacing" posts directed at her on social media.

image.altText: Man exiting sandstone building.

items.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death

children.content: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

image.altText: Rohan Dennis wearing formal attire walks along a sandstone wall, with a broadcast camera partially visible.

items.title: From 'anger and hate' to feeling 'lighter', Jack is back to being herself

children.content: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — "lighter" and with more personal belief than ever.

image.altText: Shayna Jack smiles

items.title: Driver 'didn't have time to brake' before crash that killed boy on scooter

children.content: An 11-year-old boy dies after his scooter collides with a car in Mandurah, with a witness saying they saw the boy riding down a small hill onto the road before the crash — WA's fourth road fatality over the Easter period.

items.title: Owner of dog that mauled young girl charged over attack

children.content: The owner of a dog that savaged a girl in a Perth suburb, leaving her with serious facial injuries, is charged under Western Australia's Dog Act.

image.altText: A residential street in the Perth suburb of Hillarys on a sunny day, with houses on both sides of the road.

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?

children.content: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

image.altText: Lady holding a wallet with 20 dollar bills.

items.title: Smallest town in Australia for sale as two residents decide to sell roadhouse

children.content: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

image.altText: sign reading FOX TRAP beside a road in front of a sunset

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.
A man wearing a dark polo shirt sits on a park bench with his back turned to the camera.

items.title: 'I just had to do it for me': The ambiguous loss of family estrangement

children.content: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

image.altText: A man sitting on a rock looking out towards the ocean

items.title: Quiz: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

image.altText: Elvis Presley impersonators gather for the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of Elvis Presley impersonators.

items.title: Cost of filling best-selling car up by $100 since start of Iran war

children.content: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

image.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

items.title: Team behind cult 2012 video game reunites on Grammy winner

children.content: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

image.altText: A video game character grinds their board along a large metal chain in what appears to be an underwater ruined city

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears Cyclone Narelle might be a climate disaster that results in a "double whammy" for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

items.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't reflect modern society

children.content: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

image.altText: A smiling young woman stands next to a glass cabinet of historical objects.

items.title: Matt Canavan looks to stars for 'Australia on steroids' plan

children.content: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

image.altText: Matt Canavan, smiling and wearing a suit and tie, walks down a corridor at parliament house.

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Why this photo shoot made rugby star Mel Kawa feel like a fraud

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

image.altText: Five women's Super Rugby captains pose for a photos with young girls, al with their hands on their hips.

items.title: 'No one else should go through this': Young job hunters a target in scam surge

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.
